violations.text: Cut garlic was observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit at the wok station. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved cut garlic to cooler unit for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The door to the outside, located in the side kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by small droppings in water heater closet. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Bowls (containers) are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC discarded containers during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelves and side of fryer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
Inspector observed a drip coming from the hot water heater. The drip was being collected by a container. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The door to the kitchen toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Inspector observed a stack of flattened boxes stored next to dumpster area. Area must be maintained cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The hood vents in the cooking area have an accumulation of soil and grease residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The floor in the water heater closet area has an accumulation of small droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Working containers of (dry ingredients) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Inspector observed a hanging fly glue trap with dead flies over 3 bay sink. PIC removed and disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The door to the outside, located in the (kitchen), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The cutting boards in the (kitchen) are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Establishment has an airbreak. PIC informed of airgap is needed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
The door to the (kitchen ) toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
